summary: Iteration 15 (Auto Level Match) — fully verified via Playwright against the preview URL. All 9 review-request bullets confirmed: modal opens, sweep runs at dwell=500/settle=100, Apply Match button appears post-sweep alongside Export CSV, AVG/TARGET selector toggles styling and Correction column header (→ avg / → target), every ac-correction-{id} cell shows '—' with all 16 channels at -∞ (SAFETY-FIRST invariant), ac-correctable-count shows '0 ch', ac-apply-match is disabled with label '✦ Apply Match (0)', Undo button correctly absent before apply, Apply Match button hidden during rerun and reappears after, CSV download fires with auto-capture-phy-*.csv naming, readonly mode disables ac-start (Apply Match button only renders when results exist; in readonly the sweep cannot even start, which is a stricter safety guarantee). Regression check: pn-type-pink/white/sweep, out-out_phy-0-pan-visual (Channels tab), meters-popout, open-snapshot all still present. Code inspection of computeCorrection() and the appliedUndo conditional rendering confirms math correctness and Undo button gating.
critical_code_review_comments: AutoCaptureSequence.jsx is 489 lines — still readable but approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider extracting <LevelMatchControls/> and <ResultsTable/> in a future iteration to keep the file under control.

applyLevelMatch() reads `out.gain` from state.outputs at click time which is correct, but mixing updateOutput (shallow) with updateOutputDeep (used elsewhere in the file) inside the same module is a minor inconsistency — consider standardising on updateOutputDeep for gain changes too so consumers can rely on a single mutation entry-point.

computeCorrection() is a pure helper but is re-defined at module top level (good). The clamp constants (MATCH_CLAMP_DB=12, MATCH_FLOOR_DB=-55, MATCH_DEADBAND_DB=0.3) are not exported — if the same thresholds are ever needed in tests or a settings panel, exporting them would help.

In readonly mode the Apply Match button never renders (because the sweep can't run), so the readonly invariant for that specific button is implicitly satisfied but not directly testable end-to-end. Acceptable, but worth a code comment noting the assumption.
Minor: no unit test file accompanies the new pure helper computeCorrection — adding a quick Jest test would lock in the clamp/floor/deadband behaviour against future regressions.
seed_data_creation: None — used default 16 phy outputs already in the store.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AutoCaptureSequence modal lives in MetersView (data-testid 'open-auto-capture'). Range sliders (ac-dwell, ac-settle, ac-level) need JS-dispatch input events to change (page.fill doesn't trigger React onChange on range inputs); the test harness uses a setRange helper that calls native setter + dispatches 'input'+'change'. The sweep wall-time is ~(dwell+settle+~40ms loop)*channels — for 16 phy channels at dwell=500/settle=100 it completes within ~12s; use a 20s timeout when waiting for ac-apply-match. Pan-visual lives only on the Channels tab, not Meters. AVG button bg is rgb(255,214,10) = #FFD60A when active, transparent (rgba(0,0,0,0)) when inactive.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All assertions from the iteration-15 review request matched expected behaviour.
